notes: Suite and policy bad fixtures use expected_decision=fail because the current checker reports these isolated defects as fail-class findings.
Each case targets one current command surface and preserves separate failure attribution for suite records, child closure, stale evidence, and checker-policy self-check protection.

fixtures.target_rule: A suite with a contract but no suite map must fail suite record validation.
known_limitations: This fixture covers only the missing suite-map branch.

fixtures.target_rule: A checked suite child whose member report declares no direct evidence must fail child closure validation.
known_limitations: This fixture covers only the member-report-without-direct-evidence branch.

fixtures.target_rule: A checked suite child whose evidence report is stale must fail current evidence validation.
known_limitations: This fixture covers only fixture-local stale evidence metadata.

fixtures.target_rule: A weakened checker-change policy document must fail self-check policy-artifact validation.
known_limitations: This fixture uses fixture-local policy copies and does not mutate production policy files.

skipped_checks: These fixtures intentionally do not cover static single-skill defects already represented in bad_static.
These fixtures do not replace the passing good_single_skill or good_suite fixtures.
residual_risk: The fixtures are local negative examples and do not prove broad checker behavior.
Future checker status-vocabulary changes may require refreshing expected_decision values.
claim_boundary: This suite, closure, stale-evidence, and checker-policy bad-fixture manifest covers only the listed negative fixture cases and current local files. It does not prove runtime checker execution, fixture coverage, CLI checks, tests, suite automation, package publication, code-contract validation, release readiness, or future AI behavior without separate current evidence.
